activities_projects.title: Atmospheric Pressure and Temperature Correlation
activities_projects.objective: Observe how heat affects air density and pressure
steps: Hang two paper bags in inverted position on the two ends of a wooden stick
Tie a thread in the middle of the stick to balance it like a scale
Put a burning candle below one of the bags carefully
Observe the balance of the stick as the air inside the bag warms up
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Demonstrates that warm air is lighter (less dense) and rises, creating low pressure
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of why the balance was disturbed and the direction of air movement (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mapping Global Pressure Belts
activities_projects.objective: Identify the permanent pressure systems of the Earth
steps: Mark the Equator, Tropics, and Polar circles on the map
Shade the Equatorial Low Pressure belt (Doldrums) in one color
Mark the Subtropical Highs and Subpolar Lows
Draw arrows to show the direction of 'Trade Winds' and 'Westerlies' between these belts
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the relationship between planetary winds and pressure belts
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the belt locations and correct wind direction arrows (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Mechanism of Rainfall Model
activities_projects.objective: Distinguish between Convectional, Orographic, and Cyclonic rainfall
steps: Divide a board into three sections for the three types of rainfall
Model a mountain (Orographic) and show air rising on the windward side
Model the ground heating up (Convectional) with vertical arrows
Model warm and cold air meeting (Cyclonic) with swirling arrows
Paste cotton 'clouds' at the point where condensation occurs
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies the three main types of rainfall based on how air is lifted
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of the models and correct labeling of the lifting mechanisms (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Relative Humidity Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Understand how the air's capacity to hold moisture changes
steps: Take a dry sponge and slowly pour water over it using a measuring cylinder
Note the amount of water the sponge absorbs before it starts dripping (Saturation)
Relate the sponge to 'Air' and the water to 'Moisture'
Discuss what happens to the 'capacity' if the sponge is squeezed (cooling air)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explains the concept of air saturation and condensation point
activities_projects.evaluation: Correlation between sponge capacity and air temperature/humidity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Local Wind Case Study: Loo and Sea Breeze
activities_projects.objective: Observe the difference between permanent and local winds
materials: Access to weather news or elders' interviews
steps: Research the 'Loo' (North India) or 'Sea Breeze' (Coastal areas)
Draw a diagram showing the movement of air during the day vs. night for sea/land breeze
Explain why these are called 'Local Winds' and not 'Permanent Winds'
List the time of day and the season when these winds are most active
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Differentiates between different categories of wind systems
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the breeze diagrams and local wind characteristics (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Stratosphere vs. Troposphere: The Ozone Filter
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the importance of the second layer of the atmosphere
steps: Shine a flashlight (Sun) through a clear sheet (Troposphere) onto a paper
Dabble black spots on the sheet to represent the Ozone layer in the Stratosphere
Observe how the intensity of 'UV rays' reaching the paper changes
Research why airplanes prefer flying in the lower Stratosphere
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the protective role of Ozone and the stability of the Stratosphere
activities_projects.evaluation: Description of Ozone's function and atmospheric layer properties (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Weather Instrument Station
activities_projects.objective: Learn to use and read weather measurement tools
steps: Set up the instruments in a safe outdoor location
Take readings for Temperature, Pressure, and Wind Speed at the same time for 3 days
Observe if a drop in pressure correlates with a change in weather (cloudiness)
Create a small table of your 'Meteorological' data
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops technical skills in monitoring atmospheric conditions
activities_projects.evaluation: Precision of readings and correlation of pressure with weather (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Carbon Dioxide Sink Experiment
activities_projects.objective: Understand how plants maintain the oxygen-CO2 balance
steps: Jar A: Place a lighted candle and a plant. Seal it.
Jar B: Place only a lighted candle. Seal it.
Note which candle stays lit longer and explain why
Relate this to why deforestation leads to Global Warming
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects the biological process of photosynthesis to atmospheric stability
activities_projects.evaluation: Scientific explanation of the gas exchange within the jars (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Cyclonic Circulation Model
activities_projects.objective: Observe the spiraling nature of wind in low-pressure systems
steps: Stir the water in a circular motion to create a vortex (Low pressure center)
Drop ink at the edge of the bowl and watch it get pulled toward the center
Observe the spiral pattern formed
Research why cyclones in the Northern Hemisphere rotate counter-clockwise
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes air movement in a low-pressure system (Cyclone)
activities_projects.evaluation: Sketch of the spiral pattern and identification of the center (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Longevity of the Atmosphere
activities_projects.objective: Investigate how gravity holds the atmosphere
materials: Reference data on Moon vs. Earth
steps: Research the gravity of the Moon and compare it with Earth
Explain why the Moon has no atmosphere while Earth does
Calculate the approximate height of the atmosphere where 99% of air is found
Discuss why mountain climbers carry oxygen cylinders
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes the physical factors that allow Earth to retain its air
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical comparison between Earth and the Moon's atmosphere (10 marks)
